airports.status_reason: Dubai Airports and major carriers state all flights at Dubai International (DXB) remain suspended due to UAE airspace closure as of March 2, 2026; no normal passenger operations.
Dubai Airports confirms that flight operations at Dubai World Central (DWC) are halted as part of the wider UAE airspace closure; commercial traffic effectively suspended.
Etihad had suspended all flights to and from Abu Dhabi until at least March 3, but reports on March 2 indicate Zayed International is beginning to resume some Etihad operations on limited routes.
Sharjah Airport Authority has announced suspension of all flight operations at Sharjah International Airport until further notice amid regional airspace closures.
airports.status_source_name: The Indian Express / Sharjah Airport Authority

airports.status_reason: Qatar Airways and Hamad International Airport state that flight operations remain temporarily suspended due to closure of Qatari airspace, with services to resume only after authority approval.
Muscat International Airport has not been formally closed but faces partial operational restrictions, with multiple cancellations and reroutes due to regional airspace issues.
airports.status_source_name: The Sunday Guardian / aviation and airline notices

airports.status_reason: Delhi airport remains operational but with heavy disruption; over 100 flights (mainly Middle East and some westbound long-haul) cancelled while other domestic and international services continue.
airports.status_source_name: The Indian Express / Delhi airport officials

airports.status_reason: Mumbai airport is open but affected by the Middle East crisis; around 125 flights were reportedly cancelled while many other services continue to operate with reroutes and delays.
Rome Fiumicino remains operational with only a small share of flights cancelled or rerouted due to Middle East airspace closure, representing roughly 6% of daily movements.
Munich Airport has not issued any special closure or large-scale suspension related to the Middle East situation; operations continue normally aside from prior weather-related disruptions.
